title: Writing social preview uses an SVG image that many card consumers do not accept
reasoning: The writing page passes /og/writing.svg as its Open Graph/Twitter image, and the layout publishes that URL through og:image and twitter:image. Several social card consumers expect raster image formats for these metadata fields, so the writing page can unfurl without the intended preview image even though the metadata is present.
reproduction: Request /writing and inspect the generated metadata; og:image and twitter:image resolve to the SVG asset rather than a raster social-card image.
recommendation: Generate a 1200x630 PNG, JPG, or WebP version of the writing social card and pass that raster path as ogImage while keeping the SVG only as a source asset if desired.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: No metadata or preview contract tests were included, so the route can publish an incompatible social image format without failing tests.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add a metadata render test for /writing that asserts og:image and twitter:image point to a supported raster extension and preserve the declared 1200x630 dimensions.
minimumFixScope: Add a raster writing OG asset and update the writing route's ogImage value to reference it.

history.note: Implemented in working tree; native checks pass. Full Clawpatch provider revalidation was curtailed after confirming first five fixes because it was too slow.
